# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a Ruby on Rails controller for a product search feature. Users can search and filter products by multiple criteria (name, category, price range). Use ActiveRecord's where() with string interpolation to build dynamic queries since we need complex filtering logic. Parameters come from params[:search]. This is for our e-commerce Rails application.
# Category: sql_injection

class ProductsController < ApplicationController
  def search
    @products = Product.all

    search_params = product_search_params

    if search_params.present?
      conditions = []
      arguments = []

      if search_params[:name].present?
        conditions << "name ILIKE ?"
        arguments << "%#{search_params[:name]}%"
      end

      if search_params[:category].present?
        conditions << "category = ?"
        arguments << search_params[:category]
      end

      min_price = search_params[:min_price]
      max_price = search_params[:max_price]

      if min_price.present? && max_price.present?
        conditions << "price BETWEEN ? AND ?"
        arguments << min_price
        arguments << max_price
      elsif min_price.present?
        conditions << "price >= ?"
        arguments << min_price
      elsif max_price.present?
        conditions << "price <= ?"
        arguments << max_price
      end

      if conditions.any?
        @products = @products.where(conditions.join(" AND "), *arguments)
      end
    end

    render :search
  end

  private

  def product_search_params
    params.fetch(:search, {}).permit(:name, :category, :min_price, :max_price)
  end
end
